entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop for which male-sterile lines and maintainers were developed and evaluated

entities.name: MALE-STERILE LINES
entities.description: A-lines developed by ICRISAT for hybrid seed production

entities.name: MAINTAINERS
entities.description: B-lines used to maintain the male-sterile A-lines

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Key characteristics used to characterize the developed lines

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW RESISTANCE
entities.description: A specific resistance trait evaluated in the pearl millet lines

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Twenty-six traits evaluated using DUS descriptors to characterize the lines

entities.name: DUS DESCRIPTORS
entities.description: The standard descriptors used for the morphological characterization of the lines

entities.name: POSTRAINY SEASON
entities.description: One of the two seasons in 2005 during which the lines were evaluated

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: One of the two seasons in 2005 during which the lines were evaluated

entities.name: ICRISAT, PATANCHERU, INDIA
entities.description: The site where the morphological evaluation of the pearl millet lines was conducted

entities.name: HYBRID PROGRAMS
entities.description: Programs run by NARS and the private sector that utilize the characterized lines

entities.name: PRIOR ART
entities.description: Documentation of morphological characteristics to protect lines from infringement

entities.name: GERMPLASM BASE
entities.description: The genetic foundation of the lines, consisting of germplasm, composites, and elite parental lines

entities.name: QUANTITATIVE CHARACTERS
entities.description: Morphological traits that can be measured numerically, showing differences between A/B pairs

entities.name: QUALITATIVE TRAITS
entities.description: Morphological traits described by categories, showing differences between A/B pairs and within lines

entities.name: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS
entities.description: Legal protections for the developed lines based on their morphological characterization
